ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for raisin bread (FOODON_03315763); the butter component is not separately coded. 'closest_parent' is used as the bread is the primary component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated raisin-bread-with-butter composite code; A000E (bread and similar products) is the closest defensible anchor. 'closest_parent' is used as it represents the broader category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for raisin bread with butter; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of raisin bread (approximately 35–40 g) spread with approximately 5 g of butter, providing around 132 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Raisin Bread with Butter — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05020098 is sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this source. Saturated fat is estimated from butter contribution (~53% of fat from butter at ~80% saturated fat content, yielding ~6.8 g/100g) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this composite; this estimation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 is based on white/enriched wheat bread with raisins as documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Raisin bread GI values in the database range from 63–74 for wholemeal variants and up to 78 for white-flour variants; 75 is used as a defensible central estimate for a refined-flour raisin bread. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 18.52) / 100 ≈ 13.9, based on 40 g serving providing 18.52 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.8 g/100g from butter) and negligible potassium (2 mg/100g), with borderline sodium at 288 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this food due to its high saturated fat content from butter, which contributes to arterial stiffness; if consumed, restrict to one thin slice and replace butter with avocado or a plant-based spread to reduce saturated fat and improve the potassium-to-sodium ratio.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (75) and high carbohydrate load (46.3 g/100g) from refined raisin bread, compounded by saturated fat from butter worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit this food to one small slice and pair it with a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response; choosing a wholemeal raisin bread variant and replacing butter with a MUFA-rich spread such as avocado would meaningfully reduce both glycaemic impact and insulin resistance risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~6.8 g/100g and trans fat ~0.1 g/100g from butter, with cholesterol at 28 mg/100g and sodium at 288 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, the saturated fat and trans fat contributed by butter make this food unsuitable for regular consumption; substituting butter with extra-virgin olive oil or a plant-based spread and choosing a high-fibre wholemeal raisin bread would substantially reduce LDL-raising lipid exposure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Raisin bread with butter is a sliced enriched wheat-flour bread studded with dried raisins, spread with a layer of dairy butter. The bread base is typically made from refined or semi-refined wheat flour, raisins, yeast, sugar, and salt, then baked to a soft, slightly sweet crumb with a lightly chewy texture. The butter adds a rich, creamy flavour and a smooth mouthfeel. Per 100 g, this food provides 330 kcal, 8.6 g protein, 12.84 g fat (of which approximately 6.8 g is saturated fat from butter), and 46.3 g carbohydrate with 5.24 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 288 mg/100g and calcium is notably high at 223 mg/100g, likely reflecting fortification of the bread. The glycemic index is approximately 75 (high), making it a significant glycaemic load food. Clinically, the combination of high refined carbohydrate, high saturated fat from butter, and high GI makes this food unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without careful portion control and ingredient substitution. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or snack in Singapore and internationally. The high dietary fibre content (5.24 g/100g) is a positive attribute, likely reflecting added fibre or a partially wholemeal flour base, and partially moderates the glycaemic response compared to plain white bread.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020098, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from the known fatty acid profile of butter and are documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined raisin bread, which is the closest approved source match.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (6.8 g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (3.5 g/100g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.2 g/100g), and trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100g) are estimated from the butter component contribution, as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this composite food. Estimation basis: butter constitutes approximately 5 g per 40 g serving; butter fatty acid profile per USDA FoodData Central (NDB 01001) used as reference. 2. Sugar at 0.08 g/100g is anomalously low and is likely a data artefact in the HPB source record; the carbohydrate burden from raisins and bread base remains clinically significant regardless. 3. Potassium at 2 mg/100g is extremely low and may reflect a data gap or artefact in the HPB record; this value is preserved as reported. 4. The following fields are set to -1: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food as of the accessed date. 5. This food is Western/International in origin and does not require Singaporean multilingual naming (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) under the Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules, which apply specifically to Singaporean dishes. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 40 g serving: available carbohydrate = 46.3 × 0.40 = 18.52 g; GL = (75 × 18.52) / 100 = 13.9.
